spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates `reading-flow` and `reading-order` decoupling DOM order from tab/AT order — the headline of the v137 spec.

interactive.rationale: Five buttons drive layout modes and reading-flow toggles; rendered output rearranges live.

edge_cases.rationale: Missing: contenteditable interaction, reading-flow with absolute-positioned items, screen reader narration order.

references.rationale: The see-also chromestatus link points at 5061928169472000 instead of the correct 5113638848561152 — breaks the slug self-heal recovery script.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: Replace https://chromestatus.com/feature/5061928169472000 with https://chromestatus.com/feature/5113638848561152 (the slug self-heal script needs the correct ID).

openQuestions.title: Add a screen-reader narration order indicator
openQuestions.detail: The spec change also affects accessibility tree order. Add a button that triggers an `aria-live` announcement order check so authors see the AT impact, not just the tab order.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate reading-flow with absolute positioning
openQuestions.detail: Reading-flow only re-orders flex/grid items by default; show how out-of-flow items are handled.

summary: Strong demo of the core spec change; the broken chromestatus link is the only structural fault.
